title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

ground_truth.description: The reporter manages customer records in TaskFlow. A nightly batch import job syncs customer data from their CRM. Two weeks ago, a developer updated the phone number normalizer to support international formats. The new regex-based normalizer silently truncates phone numbers with country codes longer than 2 digits (e.g., +1268 for Antigua becomes +12, +353 for Ireland becomes +35). Numbers with 1-2 digit country codes (+1 for US, +44 for UK) are fine. The affected records are all customers with phone numbers from countries with 3-4 digit country codes. The corruption happened gradually — each nightly sync overwrote good numbers with truncated ones. The old numbers are in CRM backups but not in TaskFlow's history.
ground_truth.actual_cause: The phone number normalizer regex uses a capture group that only matches 1-2 digit country codes: /^\+(\d{1,2})(\d+)$/. Numbers with 3+ digit country codes get partially matched, truncating the country code. The normalizer was updated 2 weeks ago and runs on every nightly import, silently corrupting affected numbers.
acceptable_paths: Identifying the nightly import as the corruption source and the country code pattern among affected numbers, even without finding the exact regex issue
Identifying the 2-week timeline and connecting it to a recent change in the import pipeline, even without pinpointing the normalizer
ground_truth.environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, nightly batch import from Salesforce CRM, ~2,000 customer records
ground_truth.workaround: Restore affected numbers from CRM backup; disable the nightly sync until the normalizer is fixed

expected_triage_extracts: phone numbers appear truncated, not random
pattern: affected numbers have international country codes with 3+ digits
numbers with US/UK country codes are fine
recent change to phone number normalizer
